todayLessons.reason: Closing QQQ puts as tech sell-off may be nearing exhaustion. QQQ holding 598 support with Dow up 0.5% showing sector rotation rather than broad market weakness. Small loss acceptable to avoid theta decay into weekend.

positionAnalysis.thesis: Retail turnaround, 62% P/E discount to peers
Reverse mortgage leader, Blue Owl $2.5B partnership

watchFor: AVOID: Multiple re-entries on identical thesis (AMD puts)
AVOID: Exiting profitable longs too early—AMD calls closed +7 % intraday while the stock ran to 215 after hours
AVOID: No winners booked on a day when you had a 20 % gap-down name reversing
tomorrowRules: One shot per symbol per session—no re-entry within 30 min unless new technical level breaks
Only short puts/short calls when VIX > 20 or confirmed break of prior day’s low/high with 1.5× relative volume
Take 25 % of option position off at +15 % to cover risk; trail remainder with 10 % stop

dailyLessons.gradingReason: You dodged a bullet: 0-for-3 on closed trades yet you kept every loss <4 % and walked away with only –0.5 % of equity. Good risk control, but you’re still bleeding on the day and haven’t proved an edge.
keyLessons: Stop flipping direction on the same symbol within minutes—three separate AMD-put entries that all lost means the first click was wrong; wait for a new setup or move on.
When VIX is sub-18 and futures are green, buying puts because the stock is "down a lot" is a low-probability counter-trend trade; wait for momentum confirmation or sell call strength instead.

goodPatterns: Cut losers quickly (avg –2.9 %) before theta crushed you into the weekend
Respected max-single-loss ceiling of ~0.4 % of account
badPatterns: Multiple re-entries on identical thesis (AMD puts)
Exiting profitable longs too early—AMD calls closed +7 % intraday while the stock ran to 215 after hours
No winners booked on a day when you had a 20 % gap-down name reversing

tomorrowRules: One shot per symbol per session—no re-entry within 30 min unless new technical level breaks
Only short puts/short calls when VIX > 20 or confirmed break of prior day’s low/high with 1.5× relative volume
Take 25 % of option position off at +15 % to cover risk; trail remainder with 10 % stop
dailyLessons.riskAssessment: Position sizing was sound; max risk per trade stayed under 0.5 % of equity, but frequency of trades (14) without a single win shows over-trading in a low-vol environment.
dailyLessons.emotionalCheck: No revenge size-ups, yet constant re-entry on AMD after small losses hints at "scratch-itch" trading rather than plan-based setups—work on patience.
